title: anthemis arvensis subsp. glabra
intro: Anthemis arvensis subsp. glabra, also known as field chamomile or smooth field chamomile, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family, with a preference for dry, sandy areas and waste ground. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, making it useful for treating a range of health issues. The health benefits of Anthemis arvensis subsp. glabra include reducing stress and anxiety, promoting relaxation and sleep, and soothing digestive issues such as irritable bowel syndrome. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by drying the flowers and leaves and then steeping them in hot water or using a solvent to extract the medicinal compounds.
